title: DesignReasoningTriple
description: Schema for architecture decisions captured as DESIGN_REASONING triples. Each entry documents a non-obvious decision with its context, challenge, resolution, and teaching point. Used in FLOW-XX-ARCHITECTURE-DECISIONS.json files and seeded to xiigen-rag-patterns.

decisionId.description: Unique decision identifier. Format: D-{PLAN_PREFIX}-{NNN}. Examples: D-EXT-001, D-FLOW01-001.

type.description: Category of this decision. Determines which RAG pool it is indexed into.

capability.description: The specific capability or interface being decided upon. Should be a concrete, searchable string — interface name, task type, feature name.

context.description: One to three sentences: what situation prompted this decision? Include the relevant artifact IDs (T-XXX, FLOW-XX, etc.).

proposed.description: The naive or first proposal — the approach that was initially considered. State it plainly so future sessions can recognize the same pattern.

challenge.description: Why the proposed approach is wrong or suboptimal. This is the core insight — the reasoning that eliminates the naive approach. Should be specific enough to generalize.

resolution.description: What was decided instead. Include specific artifact names, interface names, config keys, or code patterns that embody the resolution.

principleApplied.description: Which XIIGen principle (P1–P11, D-STACK-N, DNA-N, or a named rule) governed this decision. State the principle, then show how it applied.

teachingPoint.description: A single generalized lesson that can guide future sessions facing the same pattern. Should be actionable: 'Before doing X, ask Y. If Z, then W.'

examples.context: Analytics task types need Redis-style atomic counters and idempotency locks.
examples.proposed: IRedisService — name the interface after the provider
examples.challenge: IRedisService leaks provider identity into the interface name. Same mistake as 'php-wordpress' in stackCoupling keys. Callers would be coupled to a specific technology by name, even though Memcached and Valkey implement identical semantics.
examples.resolution: IScopedMemoryService — named for capability (scoped, ephemeral, fast key-value with TTL) not provider. Provider resolved via FREEDOM config.
examples.principleApplied: Fabric interfaces named for capability, never for provider. The same principle that eliminated 'php-wordpress' keys.
examples.teachingPoint: Before naming a fabric interface: can the name survive provider replacement? If renaming the provider would require renaming the interface, the interface name is wrong.
